mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers for integrating workflows into various applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool, which is necessary for managing Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Use pip to install the specific version of Langflow.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Install Langflow in the langflow namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for Langflow.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Langflow release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Langflow.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure the cluster is running with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace langflow' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the specified Helm release does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have installed Langflow in the correct namespace and check with 'helm list --namespace langflow'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.7.0, ensure to update your configurations to match the new API structure.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace langflow' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
